name: Bluefish with Fennel and Tomato Sauce (Mf)
recipeIngredient: 1/4 cup anise flavored liquor like pernod
1 cup peeled and seeded chopped tomatoes
salt and freshly ground black pepper
recipeInstructions:
1. Discard the feathery green tops of the fennel and core.
2. Cut the bulb into 1-inch pieces.
3. Heat 2 tablespoons olive oil in a 9-inch skillet over medium heat.
4. Add the fennel, garlic, orange rind, fennel seeds, liquor, salt, and pepper to the skillet.
5. Cover and simmer for 10 minutes.
6. After 10 minutes, add the tomatoes and continue to cook, covered, for another 10 minutes.
7. Remove the cover and evaporate any remaining liquid until the mixture looks syrupy.
8. Discard the orange rind.
9. Turn off the heat and stir in the butter. Season with salt and pepper; allow to cool to room temperature.
10. Season the bluefish fillets with salt and pepper, then place them skin side down in a generously buttered baking/serving dish large enough to hold the fillets in a single layer.
11. Spoon the cooled vegetable mixture over the fish and cover with foil.
12. Refrigerate for up to 12 hours.
13. To bake and serve, remove the fish from the refrigerator 30 minutes before baking.
14. Preheat the oven to 375 degrees Fahrenheit.
15. Bake covered with foil for 20 to 25 minutes until the center of the fish is opaque.
16. Remove from oven and garnish with yellow bell pepper and chives.
Yield: 4 servings
